items.title: PROJECT_SPEC.md section-8 .git-bloat decision cites a backup dir and bundle that no longer exist
items.detail: The section-8 Won't-fix locked decision ends by promising "a complete pre-filter backup sits at X:\YesAndApothecary_backup_pre_filter\ and a verified pre-filter bundle at X:	mp\yaapothecary-pre-filter-249891b.bundle". Neither exists: the backup dir is absent from the drive root and X:	mp is empty. Most likely swept by the 2026-07-06 drive-cleanup retention pass (not on its keep-list). NOT data loss: .git is intact at 865 MB (matching the decision's own figure), git rev-list still reports 951 data/ingredients/ blob entries, and commit 249891b resolves, so the strip could be redone from the live repo. What is false is the decision's stated precondition: it weighs a FUSE-workspace history-rewrite force-push against having an out-of-band restore point that no longer exists. Fork (human call, Locked Decision, never auto-editable): (a) recreate the net via 'git bundle create' at 249891b and add its path to the drive-cleanup keep-list so retention stops eating it, or (b) rewrite the clause to state that the pre-filter history lives in .git itself, which is the actual current guarantee.

items.evidence: docs/GDD.html line 4258 (Architecture tab); 0 hits for 'extends "res://' in source/; 10 class_name registrations in source/world3d/; CLAUDE.md line 37
items.prompt: HBH GDD drift, HIGH severity. docs/GDD.html line 4258 (Architecture tab) states: 'Subclasses use path-extends (extends "res://source/.../base.gd"), never class_name, to dodge Godot 4's class-registry boot timing.' This is the exact reverse of the shipped code. Verify first (line numbers drift): grep -rn 'extends "res://' source/ --include=*.gd returns 0 hits, and 10 files under source/world3d/ register a class_name. CLAUDE.md line 37 retires path-extends as a 2D-layer workaround dropped in v0.98.5 and names class_name the live pattern. Rewrite the sentence so class_name is the stated convention and the boot-timing quirk is demoted to a historical note (mirror CLAUDE.md's framing); do not delete the quirk note. Dangerous because it is the tab an agent reads to learn conventions, so following it produces wrong code. While in the file, the sibling item hbh-gdd-saveload-structural-refs-2026-07-05 covers the line-2086 save_load rewrite; landing both in one release is sensible. FUSE-truncation-sensitive: Python atomic write with read-back, assert the </html> tail survives, solo-dev voice (no em dashes, no AI vocabulary). Ship via X:\HereBeHordes\scriptselease.ps1 with a pill bump and changelog entry.

items.prompt: Ring Worker (X:\YesAndRing\src\index.js) swallows errors silently across many bare catch blocks (e.g. rateLimit() ~L50 fails open, standTable() ~L264, refreshShowCalendar() detail loop ~L1379, cron scheduled() ~L1563). observability.enabled is true in wrangler.jsonc but nothing is instrumented, so a broken daily cron or a dead upstream (TICA/Supabase) produces zero signal until users report stale data. Bar-raise findings observability-01/02/05 + reliability-01/03/04. Read-only review only; this is the fix ticket.

Community judge ratings in Ring live in Cloudflare KV only (key pattern j:<judge>, handleJudgeRating in X:\YesAndRing\src\index.js ~L662-695) with no backup, export, or Postgres mirror — months of cross-user voting data is a single point of failure if the KV namespace is lost or corrupted. Add a durable replica: either dual-write each vote to a Postgres table (like user_state) or snapshot KV to Postgres on the daily cron. Bar-raise finding data-integrity-02 (impact 5, confidence 5).

items.prompt: Ring's per-user state dual-write to KV then Postgres (handleStatePost in X:\YesAndRing\src\index.js ~L808-809) has no atomicity: if one write succeeds and the other fails, the two stores diverge with no automatic repair. Add a transient marker / repair-on-read (or background reconcile) so a partial dual-write self-heals, and log backfill failures in pgStateRead/pgStateWrite (~L746-765) instead of returning a bare ok:false. Bar-raise findings data-integrity-01 + data-integrity-03.
items.notes: 2026-07-08 queue-drain-4h: DRAINED (read-only verify at HEAD 50b2d36, apply-ready plan staged). Finding CONFIRMED: handleStatePost src/index.js L808-809 discards the pgStateWrite return at L809, so a KV-success + Postgres-fail dual-write diverges silently and the caller still gets ok:true; pgStateRead L754 / pgStateWrite L765 / lazy backfill L786 all swallow errors with no log (data-integrity-01 + 03). A read-side repair-on-read already exists (handleStateGet L785-786 backfills KV->PG on a PG miss when STATE_READ=pg) but nothing repairs the KV side and nothing logs. Staged plan to result_path: capture kvOk/pgOk in handleStatePost + structured console.error on mismatch + a transient r:<email> repair marker driving a symmetric repair-on-read, plus op-named console.error in the pgState catch blocks. KEY FINDING: the cron-budgeting half is ALREADY IMPLEMENTED and overstated by the finding - refreshShowCalendar (src/index.js L1330-1385) already does carry-over from the prior cal:tica KV record (L1333-1338), adaptive freshness (<=21-day shows daily / rest weekly via ttlMs L1341-1347), US-first+soonest-first sort, and a 30/run detail-fetch cap (L1348-1356); it landed in HEAD 50b2d36 (2026-07-06), the day BEFORE the 07-07 bar-raise flagged it. Only two halves remain live: (A) typeahead caches still 600s (10min) at L519 srch: and L554 sl:, and they skip caching empty results so a mistyped prefix re-fans every keystroke; (B) season sweeps still unconditional - handleCatSearch fans allSeasons()=9 per keystroke (L504), handleCatSlices fans classFamily x allSeasons()=27 per deep-pull (L544-546), both Promise.all fully-concurrent, growing ~annually. Staged plan to result_path: A = bump srch:/sl: TTL to 1800s + add a 120s negative-cache for empty sweeps (the higher-value win); B = findability-safe early-exit (probe newest N first / stop per class after a post-hit empty run) guarded by the 'findable two seasons ago' invariant. Recommend re-scoping the ticket (cron SATISFIED; only A+B remain) and batching A+B into ONE Ring deploy with the sibling attended Ring items (observability, community-ratings-backup, dualwrite-atomicity). Status kept pending, attempts->1.
items.prompt: Ring risks exhausting the Cloudflare Worker free-tier subrequest/CPU budget as coverage + users grow. The daily calendar cron fans 30+ show-detail fetches (X:\YesAndRing\src\index.js ~L1356), and typeahead handlers fan 9-27 concurrent TICA fetches per call (handleCatSearch ~L504, handleCatSlices ~L546). Implement adaptive cron budgeting (prioritize US + soonest shows, defer distant/stale to weekly), longer-lived search/slice caches (currently ~10min, ~L519/554), and batch/early-exit the season sweeps. Bar-raise findings cost-economics-01/02, performance-01, worker-cpu-budget-01.

Paid product storing emails, cat data, and judge votes still has no user-gated export or erasure path. Data surface mapped for the fix: KV (env.RATINGS, keyed by lowercased email) = u:<email> user state (ukey L716), sh:<email> shared-with (L819), shby:<email> shared-by (L820), jv:<email>:<judgekey> this user's votes (L683); Postgres = user_state (RLS user_id=auth.uid(), L749/759) + profiles (Stripe/subscription, migration 0003). CAUTION staged in result_path: j:<name> is an AGGREGATE tally SHARED across all users (L665/692) - erasure must DECREMENT this user's jv: contributions from the matching j: tallies, NOT delete j: outright (the prompt's 'j: votes' phrasing risks nuking every user's counts). Export = gather u:/sh:/shby: + jv:<email>:* (RATINGS.list prefix) + profile row as JSON. NOT applied: auto_safe:false live Worker feature-build; needs new gated handlers + wrangler deploy + release.ps1 + privacy-page copy + end-to-end erase/export test, all out of the no-release drain scope. Kept pending, attempts->1; batch into ONE attended Ring deploy with the sibling Ring items (observability, community-ratings-backup, dualwrite-atomicity, seed-pii).
items.prompt: Ring (a paid product storing account emails, cat names, and private judge notes) has no user-data export or deletion path. Add authenticated, user-gated DELETE /api/user/data that cascade-deletes user_state + profile + all KV records for that email (u:*, sh:*, shby:*, jv:*, j: votes) and GET /api/user/export returning the user's personal data as JSON, then document retention in a privacy page. Bar-raise finding compliance-01 (impact 4, confidence 5).

Ring's seeded demo data ships real PII to the client: X:\YesAndRing\public\data\seed.json carries real names (Pamela Ware / Alexander Abbott / Patricia Loynd, L36-38) and a real email on a live domain (pam@topspotsfarm.com, L82). seed.json is served to every visitor. Portfolio rule requires fictional example identities (Jane/John Smith, Princess Donut, jane@example.com). Confirm whether this real data is consented seed (Joey/Pam) and either document the exception explicitly or fictionalize the identities + email. Bar-raise finding brand-consistency-01.

Ring's verifyUser (X:\YesAndRing\src\index.js ~L725-738) makes a synchronous Supabase /auth/v1/user round-trip on EVERY authenticated /api request, adding latency and risking Supabase free-tier rate limits under concurrent activity. Add a short-lived (~60s) token cache keyed by token hash and/or validate the JWT signature locally via Supabase JWKS (verify exp/aud/sub), falling back to the remote call on miss. Keep the untrusted-client stance (still validate server-side). Bar-raise finding auth-token-hygiene-02 (tension: security <-> performance/reliability).

items.title: Generate or guard sitemap.xml so new project cards cannot silently miss it
items.detail: sitemap.xml is hand-maintained. Nothing under scripts/ or .github/ writes it, and the site standard does not name it as a step when a project card lands. Result: projects/cattery/, projects/gnosis/ and projects/ring/ all shipped homepage cards in c7231e8 and never reached the sitemap, so three of the newest public projects were invisible to any crawler trusting it. The missing URLs were added by hand in the 2026-07-10 drift-fix pass, which fixes the symptom, not the cause. The durable fix is one of two. Either extend scripts/update-project-pages.mjs to emit sitemap.xml from the same status/data/*.json plus projects/*/ sources it already walks, which is preferred because it runs in the Pages deploy workflow and so self-heals on every release. Or add a release.ps1 integrity guard, alongside the existing index.html and CNAME guards, that asserts every projects/*/index.html except the here-there-be-hordes redirect stub has a matching <loc> entry, and aborts if not. Design pages stay out of the sitemap on purpose because they are client-gated. Not auto-fixed because it adds a build step or a release guard, which is a structural call. Solo-dev voice, no em dashes.

All the affected files are gitignored, so this is disk clutter and not repo pollution, hence P3. The nightly audit deliberately did not mass-delete another routine's artifacts mid-flight.
